ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Matar Paneer. 'curry (food product)' FOODON_03302571 is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_description: Dishes based on legumes and cheese — curry type
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Matar Paneer. A0CKW (mixed dishes — vegetable and cheese based) is used as the closest defensible parent. Exact match unavailable.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Matar Paneer or a sufficiently close parent curry dish. Null is appropriate.

main_ingredients: cream (optional, reduced or omitted in butter-free variant)

serving.description: 1 restaurant portion (curry with paneer and peas)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Matar Paneer is approximately 300 g, comprising a generous portion of paneer cubes and green peas in a spiced tomato-onion gravy, usually accompanied by rice or bread.

nutrient_source.title: Matar Paneer — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition), composite curry entry; adapted for butter-free preparation by removing butter contribution (~5 g butter per 100 g) and adjusting fat and energy accordingly.
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN reports Matar Paneer with butter. The restaurant variant is labelled 'Free Butter' (butter-free). Nutrient values are adapted: butter contribution (~5 g per 100 g serving, ~37 kcal, ~4.1 g fat, ~2.6 g saturated fat) subtracted from the ICMR-NIN baseline. Remaining values (protein, carbohydrate, fibre, sodium, paneer-derived fat) are retained from the ICMR-NIN source. This is a defensible adaptation from a primary approved source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of green peas and paneer-based curry dishes — analogous estimate based on: (1) Green peas GI = 48 (Atkinson FS et al., 'International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008', Diabetes Care 2008); (2) Paneer GI ≈ 27 (dairy protein effect, low GI); (3) Tomato-onion spiced gravy base — low GI. Composite weighted estimate for butter-free Matar Paneer = 35.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Matar Paneer (butter-free) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 35 (low) based on the weighted contribution of green peas (GI 48, ~30% of carbohydrate load), paneer (GI ~27, dairy protein suppresses glycemic response), and the tomato-onion spiced gravy base (GI <30). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (35 × 7.4 × 3) / 100 = 7.77, rounded to 7.8. This is a defensible analogous estimate from approved GI sources.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~930 mg per restaurant serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a 300 g restaurant serving of Matar Paneer (butter-free) contributes approximately 930 mg sodium, which represents 40–47% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (7.4 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Matar Paneer (butter-free) has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 35 and a glycemic load of approximately 7.8 per 300 g serving, making it a suitable curry choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed with low-GI accompaniments such as roti or basmati rice in controlled portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Reduced saturated fat due to butter-free preparation; paneer provides moderate saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The butter-free preparation significantly reduces saturated fat compared to the standard recipe; at approximately 2.9 g saturated fat per 100 g, a 300 g serving contributes ~8.7 g saturated fat, which is within acceptable limits for most cardiovascular disease management plans when consumed as an occasional meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Matar Paneer (butter-free) is a North Indian vegetarian curry made from cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) and green peas (matar) cooked in a spiced tomato-onion gravy. In the standard recipe, butter or ghee is used to enrich the gravy; this variant omits butter entirely, resulting in a lighter dish with reduced saturated fat and caloric density. The dish is prepared by sautéing onions, ginger, garlic, and whole spices in oil, then adding tomatoes and ground spices including cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, and chilli to form a thick, aromatic gravy. Paneer cubes and cooked green peas are folded in and simmered until the flavours meld. The texture is creamy from the paneer, with a slight bite from the peas, and the flavour profile is warmly spiced, mildly tangy from tomatoes, and savoury. Nutritionally, this dish provides approximately 98 kcal per 100 g, with 6.2 g protein from paneer and peas, 5.8 g fat (predominantly from paneer), and 7.4 g carbohydrate. It is a good source of calcium (180 mg per 100 g) from paneer and provides dietary fibre (2.1 g per 100 g) from green peas. The estimated glycemic index is low (~35), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate (~310 mg per 100 g), warranting attention for those with hypertension. The dish is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian. It is commonly served with roti, naan, or basmati rice in Indian restaurants across Singapore and globally.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Matar Paneer?
Is Matar Paneer good for diabetics?
What is the GI of Matar Paneer?

Can I eat Matar Paneer if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Matar Paneer data (primary approved source) with a defensible adjustment for the butter-free preparation. No exact butter-free Matar Paneer entry exists in any approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate from approved GI sources. Confidence is medium rather than high because the adaptation introduces estimation uncertainty.
notes: 1. The food name in the input ('Mattar Panner ❤️ Free Butter') has been interpreted as 'Matar Paneer (Butter-Free)' — a North Indian curry of green peas and paneer prepared without butter. The emoji and informal spelling have been normalised. 2. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Matar Paneer (standard, with butter) by subtracting the estimated butter contribution (~5 g butter per 100 g, contributing ~37 kcal, ~4.1 g fat, ~2.6 g saturated fat). 3. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~35, low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database values for green peas (GI 48) and dairy/paneer (GI ~27). No direct GI measurement for Matar Paneer exists in the approved GI database. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this dish. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/trace in plant-oil and paneer-based preparations per ICMR-NIN; no industrial trans fats are present in this butter-free variant. 6. The restaurant is 'Jewel Of India An Indian Vegetarian Restaurant Heart Of The City at Boat Quay', Singapore. Price SGD 18.90. 7. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are closest-parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for Matar Paneer (butter-free) in any of the three ontology systems reviewed.
